channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Republicans set to give Trump big immigration win
item.description: Republicans are on the verge of giving President-elect Trump a big win on immigration to kick off his second term — and doing it with the help of Democrats while dividing the minority party on the hot-button issue.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Biden is spending his final full day in office in South Carolina. It helped him become president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden is spending his final full day in office Sunday in South Carolina, a state that holds special meaning after his commanding win in the 2020 Democratic primary there set him up to achieve his life's goal of being elected president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to be sworn in on Bible given to him by his mother, and the Lincoln Bible
item.description: On Monday, President-elect Donald Trump will use two Bibles to culminate the 60th Presidential Inauguration. He is not the first to do so, nor to use the historical Bible he chose.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: It's inauguration eve and Trump is ready to revel in his return to power
item.description: It's the day before Donald Trump's inauguration and he plans a series of Washington events that celebrate his return to power and his “Make America Great Again” movement.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Northern highlights: Alaska's energy, security policies are the guide feds need amid transition, group says
item.description: Future 49, with proponents from civilian pilots to Gov. Mike Dunleavy, is primed to launch to promote Alaskan issues and its energy, economic and national security benefits to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's departing UN envoy says US rivals will fill the vacuum if Trump abandons global leadership
item.description: The outgoing U.S. ambassador to the United Nations says she watched America’s leadership diminish in the world during Donald Trump’s first presidency and saw China fill the vacuum.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration, a triumphant return for the Republican four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capit
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Hitchhiker’s Guide to what snowball fights have to do with moving the inauguration inside
item.description: With the President-elect's Inauguration moved inside the Capitol, House and Senate offices are becoming frustrated about who may be allowed to attend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands march in Washington days before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of people from around the United States rallied in the nation's capital Saturday for women's reproductive rights and other causes they believe are under threat from the incoming Trump administration, reprising the original Women's March days before President-elect Donald
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, ASHRAF KHALIL and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY Times reporter roasted after 'unitary executive theory' flub in Trump OMB nominee story
item.description: A New York Times reporter stepped into controversy this week when critics say he fundamentally misunderstood a constitutional law doctrine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mass deportation raids could start in Chicago as soon as Tuesday: Report
item.description: Mass deportation raids threatened in Chicago could start as soon as Tuesday, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic state AGs hurry to preserve Biden gun control
item.description: Trump, in a previous campaign speech, said he would "roll back every Biden attack on the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Special session rebuttal on illegal immigration continues in Florida
item.description: The governor gave his order Monday with House and Senate leadership pushing back, and that's not the only rebuttal we've seen in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The inauguration is moving indoors. Here's what to know about the changed plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sub-freezing temperatures forecast for Monday in Washington are prompting President-elect Donald Trump to take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda. Almost everyone visiting Washington won’t be able to see it in person.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawler: Trump has right to revoke congestion pricing approval
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Congestion pricing began in New York City earlier this month. Under congestion pricing, drivers are charged a $9 toll during peak hours to enter Manhattan’s central business district at and below 60th Street. U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Blinken tells AP he's worried Trump administration may abandon key Biden foreign policy initiatives
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Outgoing Secretary of State Antony Blinken told The Associated Press that he hopes the incoming Trump administration will press forward with key points in President Joe Biden’s foreign policy, including on the Middle East and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC mayor says his meeting with Trump covered jobs and ceasefire deal, but not his criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams said his meeting with President-elect Donald Trump on Friday included discussions of bringing manufacturing jobs back to the city and the Gaza ceasefire deal — but not the mayor’s federal bribery case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem will '100% partner' with Trump if confirmed as DHS head
item.description: Kristi Noem pledges to work in tandem with Donald Trump if confirmed as the head of the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: How could a second Trump presidency affect Minnesotans? 6 experts answer your questions
item.description: On Monday, Jan. 20, Donald Trump will be inaugurated for his second term as President of the United States. As the incoming administration prepares to carry out its agenda, we asked experts to answer your questions about what the next four years could look like here in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann, Ngoc Bui, Lukas Levin, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court upholds law banning TikTok if it’s not sold by its Chinese parent company
item.description: The Supreme Court has unanimously upheld the federal law banning TikTok beginning Sunday unless it’s sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Giuliani reaches tentative settlement with Georgia election workers
item.description: Attorneys for Rudy Giuliani and two former Georgia election workers said Thursday they have reached a tentative settlement, a remarkable twist that would end the workers’ extensive litigation to collect their $146 million defamation judgment.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s Inauguration Gala: The hottest ticket in town and the biggest billionaires ball ever
item.description: And his new friends are putting their money where their mouths are — the big tech boys have donated millions to Trump’s inauguration fund.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Local reactions mixed as Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal moves forward
item.description: The Middle East and the world are celebrating the Israel-Hamas ceasefire as a possible step toward peace, but many are still cautious about the deal.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: After half a century in politics, Biden bids farewell in an Oval Office address
item.description: President Biden delivered a farewell speech to the nation from the Oval Office tonight — a chance for him to frame his own legacy one last time before he leaves the White House.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: DFLer Doron Clark, Republican Abigail Wolters win primary in Senate District 60 special election
item.description: A special primary election Tuesday narrowed the field of candidates running for a Minnesota Senate seat from nine to two.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump's Cabinet pick for attorney general is Pam Bondi. Here's what to know
item.description: Bondi is expected to receive broad support from Republicans but face sharp questions from Democrats who have concerns about whether she will protect the Justice Department from political influence.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Departing DHS Secretary Mayorkas contends he delivered border security in the end
item.description: As DHS Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas prepares to leave office, NPR sits down for an exit interview. He tells us the border is more secure now than before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Milton Guevara and Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: County Board of Supervisors votes to hold special election to fill District 1 seat
item.description: In a swift and unanimous vote, the County Board of Supervisors has agreed to a special election to fill the vacancy left behind by Nora Vargas.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin’s governor creates new violence-prevention office in wake of school shooting
item.description: Wisconsin’s Democratic governor on Tuesday created a new office dedicated to preventing gun violence, a month after a school shooting not far from the state Capitol and an idea that drew immediate opposition from Republicans who said it was misguided.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Murphy lays out ambitious goals in State of the State address
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Gov. Phil Murphy on Tuesday delivered his State of the State address for the last time with a full year at the helm ahead of him.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Democrats turn up heat on Hegseth as Republican alleges hypocrisy
item.description: Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday, the first of President-elect Trump's Cabinet candidates to face confirmation.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
